basics.description:
### 🔒 XDR Security Dashboard Notifications
Deploy a Logic App that runs on a schedule, queries Microsoft 365 Defender Advanced Hunting APIs, and sends an HTML security dashboard report via email.

**What gets deployed:**
- Logic App with daily recurrence trigger
- Office 365 API connection for sending email

⚠️ After deployment, you must authorize the Office 365 connection in the Azure Portal.

basics.name: logicAppName
basics.label: Logic App Name
basics.toolTip: Name of the Logic App resource to create

constraints.validationMessage: Must be 3-64 characters, alphanumeric, hyphens, or underscores

options.text:
Before proceeding, you must create an App Registration in Azure AD. Follow these steps:

1. Go to Azure Portal → Azure Active Directory → App registrations → New registration
2. Name it (e.g. 'XDR-Dashboard-Reader') and click Register
3. Copy the **Application (client) ID** — you'll need it below
4. Go to **Certificates & secrets** → New client secret → set an expiry → click Add
5. Copy the **Secret Value** immediately (it won't be shown again) — you'll need it below
6. Go to **API permissions** → Add a permission → APIs my organization uses → search 'Microsoft Threat Protection'
7. Select **Application permissions** → check **AdvancedHunting.Read.All** → click Add permissions
8. Click **Grant admin consent** for your organization

The Tenant ID is automatically detected from your Azure subscription.

elements.name: clientId
elements.label: App Registration Client ID
elements.toolTip: The Application (client) ID from step 3 above. Found in Azure AD → App registrations → your app → Overview page.

elements.name: clientSecret
elements.toolTip: The secret Value (not the Secret ID) from step 5 above. Found in Azure AD → App registrations → your app → Certificates & secrets.

options.text: Configure the email sender and recipient for the security dashboard report. After deployment, you must go to the API connection resource and click 'Edit API connection' → 'Authorize' to sign in with the sender mailbox.

elements.name: emailRecipient
elements.label: Recipient Email Address
elements.toolTip: The email address that will receive the daily XDR security dashboard report (e.g., soc@company.com or your-team-dl@company.com)

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a valid email address

elements.name: emailSender
elements.label: Sender Email Address
elements.toolTip: The email address that sends the report. Must be a valid Office 365 mailbox in your tenant that you can sign into during the post-deployment authorization step.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be a valid email address

elements.name: office365ConnectionName
elements.label: Office 365 Connection Name
elements.toolTip: A display name for the API connection resource created in your resource group. This is just a resource name — you can leave the default 'office365' or use something like 'office365-soc-reports' for clarity.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be 1-64 alphanumeric characters, hyphens, or underscores

options.text: Configure how often the security dashboard report is generated and sent. Default is once per day.

elements.name: recurrenceInterval
elements.label: Run Every
elements.toolTip: How often the Logic App runs

elements.name: recurrenceTimeZone
elements.label: Time Zone
elements.toolTip: Windows time zone name for the schedule (e.g., 'UTC', 'Pacific Standard Time', 'Israel Standard Time')
